name: Alto Gállego — circular from the Embalse de Peña
name_original: Alto Gállego — circular desde el Embalse de Peña
region_intro: Touring cycling around Las Peñas De Riglos offers a diverse landscape at the foothills of the Pyrenees, characterized by its unique conglomerate rock formations known as the Mallos de Riglos. The region features a mix of rugged mountain peaks, deep canyons like Foz de Escalete, and expansive valleys, alongside forests and rivers. This varied terrain provides a range of cycling experiences, from routes on paved surfaces to challenging trails with unpaved and demanding segments. The area's natural monuments and dramatic scenery create a distinctive backdrop for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Las Peñas De Riglos The most popular touring cycling route is Alto Gállego — circular from the Embalse de Peña, a 28.1 miles (45.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views around the Embalse de Peña. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of San Felices – Mallos de Agüero loop from Agüero, a difficult 21.1 miles (34.0 km) path. This route provides views of the iconic Mallos de Agüero rock formations. Local touring cyclists also love the Las Piedras de Agüero – Mallos de Agüero loop from Riglos-Concilio, a 37.8 miles (60.8 km) trail leading through the dramatic Mallos de Agüero landscape, often completed in about 6 hours 4 minutes. Touring cycling around Las Peñas De Riglos is defined by towering rock formations, deep canyons, and varied mountain terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging routes. The routes in Las Peñas De Riglos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Las Peñas De Riglos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Las Peñas De Riglos?
A: There are 10 touring cycling routes in Las Peñas De Riglos, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Las Peñas De Riglos?
A: The terrain in Las Peñas De Riglos is quite varied, ranging from mostly paved surfaces to challenging trails with unpaved and demanding segments. You'll encounter a mix of asphalt, tracks, and trails, often with significant elevation changes as you navigate the foothills of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners or families?
A: While most routes in Las Peñas De Riglos are rated as difficult, there is one moderate route available: &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980015875/alto-gallego-circular-from-the-embalse-de-pena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Gállego — circular from the Embalse de Peña&lt;/a&gt;. This 45.2 km route takes approximately 3 hours 18 minutes and offers scenic views around the Embalse de Peña, making it a good option for those seeking a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced touring cyclists, Las Peñas De Riglos offers several demanding routes. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005990/las-piedras-de-aguero-mallos-de-aguero-loop-from-riglos-concilio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Piedras de Agüero – Mallos de Agüero loop from Riglos-Concilio&lt;/a&gt;, a 60.8 km trail with over 1600 meters of elevation gain, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005704/view-of-san-felices-mallos-de-aguero-loop-from-aguero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of San Felices – Mallos de Agüero loop from Agüero&lt;/a&gt;, a 34.0 km path featuring over 1200 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Las Peñas De Riglos are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980015875/alto-gallego-circular-from-the-embalse-de-pena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Gállego — circular from the Embalse de Peña&lt;/a&gt;, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31501621/bike-loop-from-villalangua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Villalangua&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while cycling in Las Peñas De Riglos?
A: The region is famous for its dramatic natural monuments. You'll cycle past the iconic &lt;strong&gt;Mallos de Riglos&lt;/strong&gt;, towering conglomerate rock formations, and may even spot griffon vultures nesting in their niches. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31512559/foz-de-escalete-garoneta-nueva-bridge-loop-from-santa-maria-y-la-pena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Escalete – Garoneta Nueva Bridge loop from Santa María y La Peña&lt;/a&gt; will take you through stunning canyons.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich with panoramic views. You can find stunning vistas at highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cielo - Panoramic View&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5284678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puy Fonguera Summit and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Puy Moné&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes offer direct views of the impressive Mallos de Riglos and the surrounding Aragonese Pre-Pyrenees.

Q: What cultural or historical attractions are near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, the area offers cultural gems. The nearby &lt;strong&gt;Castillo de Marcuello&lt;/strong&gt; provides spectacular panoramic views, and the well-preserved Romanesque &lt;strong&gt;Loarre Castle&lt;/strong&gt; is a significant historical site. Charming villages often feature Romanesque churches, such as the Chapel of San Martín in Riglos.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Las Peñas De Riglos?
A: The komoot community highly rates touring cycling in Las Peñas De Riglos, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Reviewers often praise the breathtaking scenery, particularly the dramatic Mallos de Riglos, and the diverse terrain that offers both scenic roads and challenging trails.

Q: Are there places to stay or rest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, for those looking to extend their adventure or find a place to rest, there are several huts and shelters in the vicinity. Options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5284691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio de la Artica&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5930369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio La Cochera&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2622143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonfría Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, among others.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Las Peñas De Riglos?
A: Las Peñas De Riglos offers a diverse landscape that can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential winter snows in higher elevations. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.
tips: Bridge that crosses the Gállego river as it passes through the town of Anzánigo and from where it descends towards the Peña Reservoir. The population is enclosed in the area called Alto Gállego.
Channel that takes us from the small town of Anzánigo to the center of Javierrelatre, running along the Gállego riverbed. It does so under the southern slope of the Campaniella mountain.
This bridge, near the mouth of the Garona river in the Gállego, parallel to a railway bridge (in the image) gives access to the special Garona valley.
